meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to generate passive income and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no project ideas' paralysis requires shifting from invention to utility. By building micro-SaaS tools that solve specific, high-friction problems for niche audiences, you create passive income streams. Use Antigravity to scaffold architecture and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, transforming your academic skills into a scalable, revenue-generating portfolio asset.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the greatest enemy of the aspiring engineer. You have the technical stack, the curiosity, and the drive, yet you remain shackled by the paralysis of not knowing what to build. It feels like you're standing on the edge of a career, waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never seems to strike.

But the secret isn't inspiration; it's observation. Passive income isn't built on revolutionary inventions—it's built on boring, repetitive problems that people are already paying to solve. By leveraging modern AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can bypass the tedious boilerplate and launch functional, value-driven applications in a weekend. Your first project isn't about changing the world; it's about shipping code that works.

steps.title: Identify a Micro-Niche Problem
steps.description: Find a small, underserved community on Reddit or Twitter and identify a repetitive task they complain about. Your goal is to build a tool that automates just that one task.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these 50 Reddit comments from r/excel regarding spreadsheet automation and identify the top three recurring pain points that can be solved with a simple Python script.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research --query 'common automation pain points for digital marketers'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular project structure. Avoid over-engineering; focus on a single input-output flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a FastAPI boilerplate project structure for a micro-SaaS that takes user input, processes it via OpenAI API, and returns a formatted PDF report.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'init project structure with FastAPI and Pydantic models'

steps.title: Build the Core Utility Logic
steps.description: Implement the core logic using Claude Code. Focus on handling errors and edge cases early to ensure reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust function to handle API rate limiting and retries for a service that processes text using GPT-4o-mini.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'implement error handling for OpenAI API calls in main.py'

steps.title: Integrate Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your application so you can accept payments. Keep the implementation simple—a basic 'pay-per-use' model works best for beginners.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe checkout integration for a Python backend that verifies payment success before triggering the core utility function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add stripe payment session integration to the checkout endpoint'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Deploy your app to a platform like Railway or Render so it is live and accessible. Your project is now a real-world asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Dockerfile and a production-ready configuration for deploying a FastAPI app on a cloud platform.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate Dockerfile and deployment script for production'

pro_tips: Always build for a specific audience; if you try to build for everyone, you build for no one.
Use 'feature-flagging' early so you can disable buggy features without redeploying the entire application.
Document your build process in a public GitHub repository; this is 10x more valuable than a resume for recruiters.
Prioritize speed of deployment over code elegance; you can refactor later once you have your first paying user.
Monitor your API costs daily; passive income disappears quickly if your infrastructure costs exceed your subscription revenue.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on distribution—post about your tool in the communities where you found the problem, and gather feedback to iterate.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Don't let your ideas stay trapped in your head—transform them into a portfolio that commands respect and generates real revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
